assessment.headline: The Class of 2024 achieved an 89% employment rate within 12 months, up from 86% for the Class of 2023. UAE-based organizations — led by G42 and TII — are emerging as the top employers, with 34% of graduates remaining in the country. The first cohort is now large enough for the data to tell a meaningful story about where MBZUAI graduates end up and what they go on to do.
whereWeStand: 312 total graduates across three completed cohorts. The Class of 2024 (114 graduates) is the largest and most recent with complete data.
G42 and TII are the top two hiring organizations — both directly connected to MBZUAI's research mission through JAIS and related projects.
34% of graduates stay in the UAE, up from 31%. This is the metric government stakeholders watch most closely.
The first MBZUAI graduate has been appointed to a tenure-track faculty position (King's College London), and the first graduate-founded startup has received pre-seed funding through Hub71.
The Alumni Service Team has been formally established — organizing career networking events, maintaining the alumni directory, and building employer partnership pipelines.
The Alumni Advisory Council was inaugurated in February 2026 with 15 founding members across industry, academia, and government.
whatsNext: Class of 2025 career outcomes survey is in the field — 82% response rate so far, preliminary data expected April 2026. **Owner: Career Services Director**
Career Services is developing an employer partnership program to formalize relationships with top hiring organizations. **Owner: Career Services Director**
The alumni base is now large enough (312) to warrant structured engagement — the Alumni Advisory Council was announced in February. **Owner: Alumni Relations Director**
Alumni engagement strategy being formalized — quarterly meetups, mentorship matching program, and alumni-as-guest-lecturer pipeline. **Owner: Alumni Relations Director**

historicalCohorts.note: Survey in field — preliminary data expected April 2026

alumni.name: Khalid Al-Nuaimi
alumni.achievementHeadline: Leading G42's Arabic language model fine-tuning team
alumni.careerPath: Joined G42 immediately after graduation. Within 18 months, promoted to lead a team of 6 working on JAIS fine-tuning for enterprise applications.
alumni.achievement: Led the development of G42's Arabic enterprise AI assistant, now deployed across 3 UAE government ministries. The project directly built on his MBZUAI thesis on Arabic instruction-tuning.
alumni.mbzuaiConnection: His MSc thesis on Arabic instruction-tuning under Prof. Choudhury became the foundation for the G42 enterprise product. He remains a guest lecturer at MBZUAI.

alumni.name: Dr. Priya Mehta
alumni.achievementHeadline: First MBZUAI graduate to secure a tenure-track faculty position
alumni.careerPath: Completed her PhD in 2.5 years with 8 publications. Received offers from King's College London and University of Edinburgh. Chose KCL.
alumni.achievement: Appointed as Lecturer (tenure-track) in the Department of Informatics at King's College London, making her the first MBZUAI graduate to join a faculty at a top-100 university.
alumni.mbzuaiConnection: Her PhD work on neural scene understanding with Prof. Jia Deng produced 3 CVPR papers and established her as an emerging voice in 3D computer vision.

alumni.name: Omar Hassan
alumni.achievementHeadline: Co-founded Abu Dhabi AI startup — pre-seed funded through Hub71
alumni.careerPath: After graduating, joined TII briefly before leaving to co-found Arabiya AI, a startup building Arabic-first enterprise search. Accepted into Hub71 incubator in January 2026.
alumni.achievement: Arabiya AI raised AED 2M in pre-seed funding through Hub71. The product uses Arabic NLP techniques developed during his MBZUAI research to provide enterprise document search in Arabic.
alumni.mbzuaiConnection: His MSc project on Arabic semantic search directly became the technical foundation for Arabiya AI. He credits MBZUAI's proximity to the Abu Dhabi startup ecosystem as a key factor.

alumni.name: Soo-Jin Park
alumni.achievementHeadline: Joined Google Brain's foundation models team in Zurich
alumni.careerPath: Graduated with distinction. Published 2 papers during MSc. Recruited directly by Google Brain during an internship in her final semester.
alumni.achievement: Working on Google's next-generation multilingual foundation models. Co-authored a paper at ICML 2026 within 6 months of joining.
alumni.mbzuaiConnection: Her MBZUAI work on efficient multilingual model training aligned directly with Google Brain's priorities. Her advisor Prof. Solorio facilitated the internship connection.

alumni.name: Fatima Al-Shamsi
alumni.achievementHeadline: Advising Mubadala portfolio companies on AI implementation
alumni.careerPath: Joined Mubadala's technology investment team after graduation. Works at the intersection of AI technical evaluation and investment strategy.
alumni.achievement: Within 6 months, became the team's primary technical advisor for evaluating AI startups in Mubadala's portfolio. Led the AI due diligence for two investments totalling AED 50M+.
alumni.mbzuaiConnection: The Applied AI program's emphasis on business applications of AI was unique preparation for the investment role. Emirati national in a high-profile sovereign wealth fund — strong government narrative.

alumni.name: Ahmed Al-Qassimi
alumni.achievementHeadline: Leading ADDA's AI-enabled government services program
alumni.careerPath: Joined ADDA through the National Experts Program AI track. Promoted to senior engineer within one year.
alumni.achievement: Leading the implementation of AI-powered document processing for 4 government service centers, reducing processing times by an estimated 60%.
alumni.mbzuaiConnection: His MBZUAI capstone project on Arabic document understanding was directly relevant to the ADDA role. Connected through NEP AI track hosted at MBZUAI.

activities.title: Alumni Advisory Council Launch
activities.description: Inaugurated with 15 founding members representing G42, TII, Google, Mubadala, and academic institutions. Council will advise on curriculum relevance, employer partnerships, and alumni engagement strategy.

activities.title: Career Networking Mixer — Abu Dhabi Tech Week
activities.description: Co-hosted with Hub71. 45 alumni attended alongside 12 employer partners. Resulted in 8 informational interview connections for Class of 2025 students.

activities.title: Alumni Mentorship Pilot
activities.description: Launched a 6-month pilot pairing 20 current students with alumni mentors in industry. Participating mentors include G42, TII, and Google employees.

activities.title: Alumni Directory & Platform
activities.description: Comprehensive alumni tracking system launched with 280 of 312 alumni profiles verified (90% coverage). Includes employment history, willingness to mentor, and event participation.

awards.title: Rising Star Award — Khalid Al-Nuaimi
awards.description: Recognized for leading G42's Arabic language model fine-tuning team within 18 months of graduation.

awards.title: Entrepreneurship Award — Omar Hassan
awards.description: First MBZUAI graduate to found a venture-backed startup (Arabiya AI, Hub71).

awards.title: Academic Excellence Award — Dr. Priya Mehta
awards.description: First MBZUAI graduate to secure a tenure-track position at a top-100 university (King's College London).

dataNote: Data shown reflects Class of 2024 outcomes (survey closed December 2025, 82% response rate). Class of 2025 preliminary data expected April 2026.
